steps.verdict: MCP accepted, broker published, but custom service did NOT apply — physical state stayed off
normal: single-channel switch does not retain status/<device>
device online, 1 usage item (switch_1 → endpoint custom/26067540-57076820)
ANOMALY: switch_1 reported off after we sent {status:on} — wire-property mismatch
live cache confirms the state change

bug_found.summary: homed_set_device did NOT auto-translate single-channel-style command {status:on} to the actual wire-property {status_1:on} for garland_window
bug_found.context: homed_list_devices shows garland_window with name=garland_window, id=26067540-57076820, and homed_list_exposes shows it has common.items=[switch_1]. The UI property is switch_1, but the MCP server apparently did not derive the wire-property=status_1 from the expose declaration and instead treated it as a generic single-channel switch (wire=status).
bug_found.expected_behavior: Per homed_set_device docs: for multi-channel switch devices (UI id 'switch_N') the wire property is 'status_N' (so endpoint='custom/Svet', property='switch_13', value='on' sends {"status_13":"on"} to 'td/custom/Svet'). For single-channel switch/lock/valve exposes (UI id 'switch') the wire property is 'status'.
bug_found.observed_behavior: We called property='status' (the single-channel default) but the device is multi-channel (switch_1), so the broker payload became {"status":"on"} and the custom service ignored it. The fix: either the MCP resolver should detect 'switch_1' in exposes and use 'status_1', or callers must explicitly use property='status_1'.
next_steps: Agent-coder: re-read homed_set_device buildPayload() in mcp-server/internal/mcp/tools.go and confirm the resolveWireProperty() function correctly falls through to 'status_1' for switch_N → wire status_N
Agent-logger: pull homed-mcp.log for 2026-06-08 18:51:2x and grep for td/custom/garland_window to confirm the actual published payload shape
Add a unit test in mcp-server/internal/mcp that exercises endpoint=custom/garland_window, property=switch_1 and asserts the outbound payload is {status_1:on}, not {status:on}
